data.title: TG Therapeutics rises after trial setback for high dose version of Roche’s MS drug
data.description:
Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) (OTCQX:RHHBF) announced Wednesday that a Phase 3 trial for a high-dose version of its multiple sclerosis therapy Ocrevus (ocrelizumab) failed in a Phase 3 trial, sending the shares of rival MS drug developer TG Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TGTX) higher.

The Swiss drugmaker, with its local rival Novartis (NYSE:NVS), dominated the market until the U.S. biotech TGTX won FDA approval in 2022 for ublituximab under the brand name Briumvi for adults with MS, a disease of the central nervous system.

Roche (OTCQX:RHHBF) said that its Phase 3 MUSETTE trial didn’t reach the primary endpoint as a high-dose regimen of intravenously delivered Ocrevus failed to outperform the currently approved Ocrevus IV 600 mg version in patients with relapsing MS.

However, the high-dose version indicated a tolerability profile consistent with the currently approved Ocrevus, leading to no new safety concerns.  

“These findings reaffirm that the current OCREVUS IV 600 mg is optimally dosed to significantly slow disability progression,” said Levi Garraway, Roche’s (OTCQX:RHHBY) medical chief. The company expects to share the full results from the study at an upcoming medical event.

data.title: Mastercard and MoneyGram in pact for domestic, cross-border money movement
data.description:
Mastercard (NYSE:MA) and MoneyGram will work together to facilitate digital money movement domestically and across borders, the companies said on Wednesday.

Through Mastercard Move, MoneyGram customers will be able to use any U.S.-issued Mastercard (NYSE:MA) card to send funds cross border to 38 eligible receiving markets. The system also supports the ability for MoneyGram customers to receive money through almost 10B endpoints worldwide, they said. Additional cross-border payment capabilities through MoneyGram are expected throughout 2025.

The alliance builds on the MoneyGram network, which reaches 200 countries and territories with ~450,000 retail locations and 5B digital endpoints. Mastercard Move (MA) has access to more than 95% of the world's banked population, reaching more than 180 countries and supporting more than 150 currencies, the companies said.

"By integrating Mastercard Move into MoneyGram's vast network, we're building in speed and added peace of mind into every transaction so that critical funds can get into hands when and where it's needed," said Chiro Aikat, co-president, United States, Mastercard (MA).

Mastercard (MA) stock slipped 0.7% in midday Wednesday trading.

MoneyGram International went private in June 2023 when it was acquired by private equity firm Madison Dearborn Partners.

data.title: Nintendo Switch 2 details revealed at Direct event; Here's all you need to know
data.description:
Nintendo (OTCPK:NTDOY) (OTCPK:NTDOF) on Wednesday said the Switch 2 console will be available for purchase on June 5 for $449.99 in the United States.

The Switch 2 comes with a larger screen and new Joy-Con 2 controllers, each of which can now be used as a mouse in compatible games, the company said during its Direct presentation.

A bundle pack, which includes the new Mario Kart World game, will be available for $499.99 in the U.S. and will also be launched on the same day.

In the UK, Switch 2 will be starting from £395.99 and €469.99 in Germany. The latest console from the Japanese gaming giant will be available for preorder in North America on April 9 and in Europe on April 8.

Key Takeaways

Inside The Box 

The Nintendo Switch 2 console, L+R controllers, a docking station, and an AC adapter, among other things.

The Console

The Switch 2 comes with a 7.9-inch LCD screen with 1080p resolution, but games can be played in 4K on TV with up to 120 fps via the dock connection. The console has 256GB of storage, which is expandable only via microSD Express cards.

The new NS2 Joy-Cons use a magnetic locking system, and its game cartridges are red.

New Features

The right Joy-Con comes with a "C button," which enables the GameChat feature, where users can communicate with each other while playing. The feature will have open access until March 31, 2026, the company said.

Nintendo also revealed GameShare, which will allow players to share and play the same game with others who do not have it. 

"If one person has a Nintendo Switch 2 system and a game compatible with GameShare, that person can invite others to temporarily play the same game together via local play with their Nintendo Switch 2 or Nintendo Switch system," the company said. 

The Games

The console will be able to play three types of games: Nintendo Switch 2 games or the exclusives, old Nintendo Switch games, and Nintendo Switch 2 edition games, which are basically games released on the predecessor but optimized and upgradable via expansion packs for a fresher gameplay experience.

The company highlighted that the new console may play compatible physical and digital games released on its predecessor, but some games may not be supported or fully compatible.

New titles: Mario Kart World, Donkey Kong Bananza, FC Sports, Madden NFL, Cyberpunk 2077: Ultimate Edition, NBA 2K, WWE 2K, Hogwarts Legacy, Street Fighter 6, Project 007, and Borderlands 4, among others.

Switch 2 edition titles: Super Mario Party Jamboree, The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild, The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom, Kirby and the Forgotten Land + Star-Crossed World, Metroid Prime 4: Beyond, and Pokémon Legends Z-A, among others.

New Gadgets

The company is launching an NS2 Pro controller and a new camera for video chats within the game.It also announced a new GameCube controller, which will allow users to play classic games. The new gadgets will be available on the same day as launch day.

data.title: Amazon submits a late bid to buy TikTok as the sale deadline approaches
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) has entered the bidding war for TikTok ahead of the Saturday deadline, according to a report from The New York Times. A formal bid arrived in a letter addressed to Vice President JD Vance and Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick. However, sources indicate that parties involved in the talks do not appear to be taking Amazon's (NASDAQ:AMZN) bid seriously. 

Policymakers in both parties have expressed deep national security concerns over the app's Chinese ownership, which led to a law that forces a sale. Subsequently, President Trump delayed the enforcement of that law until April 5, even after it was unanimously upheld by the Supreme Court. Trump is expected to meet with White House officials later in the day to discuss TikTok’s fate.

Companies interested in buying or taking a stake in TikTok also include Oracle (ORCL), Rumble (RUM), and Perplexity AI. Former Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin is also leading an investor group with plans to acquire TikTok. Meanwhile, Kevin O'Leary and Frank McCourt are in the mix. O'Leary, known from Shark Tank, and McCourt, a billionaire real estate tycoon, have joined forces under "The People's Bid for TikTok." Looking back, Microsoft (MSFT) showed interest in TikTok in 2020 and Walmart (WMT) is also believed to have explored the social media platform.

Shares of Amazon (AMZN) were up 1.4% at 12:12 p.m. after starting the session in negative territory.

data.title: CME to launch spot-quoted futures in June for crypto, equity index markets
data.description:
CME Group (NASDAQ:CME) is planning to roll out spot-quoted futures contracts on June 30, allowing market participants to trade benchmark crypto and equity index futures in spot-market terms, it said on Wednesday.

Pending regulatory review, the contracts will be available for bitcoin (BTC-USD) and ether (ETH-USD) -- the two largest crypto assets -- as well as across four major U.S. stock indices, including the S&P 500, Nasdaq-100, Russell 2000 and Dow Jones Industrial Average.

"In response to growing retail demand for smaller-sized, longer-duration products, these capital-efficient contracts – designed with similar features of perpetual contracts –  will provide greater precision and market accessibility to clients," Tim McCourt, global head of Equities, FX and Alternative Products at CME Group, said in a statement.

The contracts "will provide retail traders more flexibility by allowing them to either maintain the position in accordance with their longer-term view, or to more easily trade in and out of positions, without having to roll positions frequently," he added.

data.title: Safran set to win EU approval for Collins Aerospace deal - Reuters
data.description:
EU antitrust authorities are expected to approve Safran's (OTCPK:SAFRY) $1.8 billion purchase of Collins Aerospace's flight controls unit. 

The European Commission's approval for the deal is conditioned on Safran (OTCPK:SAFRY) selling its electromechanical actuation business in North America to address the EU's antitrust worries, according to a Reuters report on Wednesday, which citied people familiar with the matter. 

The news comes after the U.K.'s antitrust watchdog on Friday said it will refer the Safran deal to an in-depth, Phase 2 investigation, unless the parties offer further remedies.

Collins Aerospace is a subsidiary of RTX Corp. (NYSE:RTX) and was formed in 2018 from the merger of Rockwell Collins and UTC Aerospace Systems; Collins agreed to sell its actuation and flight controls business unit to Safran in July 2023 for $1.8B.

data.title: BlackRock lands on U.K. crypto register amid ETP push
data.description:
BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) secured registration on the U.K.'s crypto register, enabling the world's largest asset manager to act as an arranger for its iShares Digital Assets AG business, which issues crypto-focused exchange-traded products.

Established in 2020, the Financial Conduct Authority's crypto register requires crypto-linked firms to register and meet U.K. anti-money laundering standards before serving customers in the country.

Now that it's been added to the list, BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) will be able to "arrange the execution of transactions in cryptoassets" for ETP subscriptions and redemptions between issuers and authorized participants, though it cannot onboard new customers, the regulator's website showed.

The company will not, however, have the ability to "operate any machine which utilises automated processes to exchange cryptoassets for money or money for cryptoassets."

The move comes more than a year after the BlackRock (BLK) rolled out the iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT), which easily became the largest U.S.-listed spot bitcoin (BTC-USD) exchange-traded fund, now boasting $48.8B in assets under management.

Last month, BLK listed its first bitcoin (BTC-USD) ETP in Europe.

The ETP launches are "expanding our presence with more investors" globally, said Larry Fink in his annual chairman's letter to investors. He noted that "more than half of demand for our Bitcoin ETP has been from retail investors, and three-quarters of those investors had never owned an iShares product before."

data.title: Trump tells insiders Elon Musk will step back soon from lead government role: Politico
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has told people in his inner circle that Elon Musk will step back from his role as a governing partner in the coming weeks, Politico reported Wednesday. 

Trump and Musk in recent days had decided Musk will soon to return to his businesses and take on a supporting role in government, the report said, citing three unnamed Trump insiders. Trump remains pleased with Musk and his Department of Government Efficiency initiative, the report said. 

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares rose 2.8% to $275.75 in late-morning trade. They were lower earlier after Tesla’s Q1 deliveries plunged, with the EV maker posting 336,681 deliveries, below the consensus estimate of 377,000 vehicles. 

Broadly, stocks moved higher (SP500)(COMP:IND)(DJI) after muted moves ahead of the Trump administration’s announcement about potential reciprocal tariffs on trading partners worldwide.

data.title: T, TMUS top chart as communication services gainers in Q1; GOOGL, DIS among losers
data.description:
Communication Services Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLC), which tracks S&P 500 Communication services sector, was one of the worst performers during the first quarter, showing a decline of nearly 1%.

The communication services sector, which consists of stocks including Alphabet (GOOG), Meta Platforms (META), Disney (DIS) and AT&T (T), is one of the sectors other than Information technology (XLK), consumer discretionary (XLY) and Industrials (XLI) that recorded a fall during the quarter. This is in comparison to the over 4% fall in the broader S&P500 Index.

Investors saw a volatile first quarter, with markets mostly remaining in the red due to uncertainties over tariff policies, higher inflation, geopolitical tensions and corporate earnings from companies.

Alphabet (GOOG) fell over 6% in reaction to the tech giant’s fourth quarter results. Even though the company narrowly topped profit expectations, it posted a few lackluster unit performances outside core beats in search and YouTube sales. On the other hand, Netflix’s (NFLX) shares hit record high, after investors were left impressed by the streaming giant’s much higher user growth than expected.

Industries Q1 Performance

Media & Entertainment fell about 10% in the first quarter, while Telecommunication Services gained 19%.

U.S. stock fund flow in and out of the sector was mostly average throughout the year, with noticeable dips during the month of March. The Communication Services-focused ETF had a net outflow of $399 million.

What are the top 5 movers in Q1?

  • Gainers: AT&T (T) +25.7%.
  • T-Mobile US (TMUS) +20.8%.
  • Fox (FOX) +15.3%.
  • Take-Two (TTWO) +13.9%.
  • Verizon (VZ) +13.8%.
  • Losers: Alphabet (GOOGL) -18.14%.
  • Walt Disney (DIS) -11.8%.
  • Omnicom (OMC) -7.9%.
  • Interpublic Group of Companies (IPG) -6.9%
  • Match Group (MTCH) -4.9%.

What Quantitative Measures say?

XLC received a Buy rating from Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, with a score of 4.33 out of 5. The sector got A+ for liquidity, expenses and momentum. However, it got a B+ for dividend and risk prospect.

What analysts expect?

One analyst surveyed by Seeking Alpha in the last 90 days gave the XLC a Buy rating.

According to FactSet analyst John Butters, Communication Services is one of the sectors which is expected to see the largest price increases in the next 12 months, with earnings and revenue for XLC expected to grow nearly 15% and 8% for CY25, respectively.

A recent Seeking Alpha analysis by Komal Sarwar noted “XLC offers a balanced portfolio of growth and defensive stocks, with lower downside risk and higher risk-adjusted returns” compared to its peers. However, Sarwar added that macroeconomic trends and consumers spending power are also a key to the sector's performance.

data.title: Qualcomm ticks up as chipmaker officially unveils Snapdragon 8s Gen 4
data.description:
• Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) shares ticked up fractionally on Wednesday as the San Diego-based chipmaker officially unveiled its Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 chipset.
  • The chipset, which is believed to use Taiwan Semiconductor's (TSM) 4 nanometer process, will offer support for Wi-Fi 7, Bluetooth 6.0, UFS 4.0 and other technologies. It
  • also offers a 49% improvement on graphics compared to the previous generation Snapdragon 8s Gen 3, Qualcomm added.
  • Qualcomm also said the new Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 has artificial intelligence-related improvements compared to the previous generation, with the system-on-a-chip showing a 44% performance improvement due to a better neural processing unit. 
  • The Cristiano Amon-led company did not say when the Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 chipset would be available, but it is expected to be used by several Android handset makers in the near future.
